calendar.matters:
Beneficiary Michelle Nemoit'S Petition (1)To Compel Accounting (2)Demand Information; And (3) To Restrain Trustee From Using Trust Assets To Defend L
TENTATIVE RULING: PRELIMINARY TENTATIVE RULING:
Counsel for the petitioner still has not provided a proposed order to the court (assigned examiner), suspending the trustee, as directed by the judicial officer on 4/7/26.
EXAMINER: HELEN TROWBRIDGE

Beneficiary Michelle Nemoit'S Petition (1)To Compel Accounting (2)Demand Information; And (3) To Restrain Trustee From Using Trust Assets To Defend L
Off Calendar May-05-2026 Continued To Jun-10-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Beneficiary Michelle Nemoit'S Petition (1)To Compel Accounting (2)Demand Information; And (3) To Restrain Trustee From Using Trust Assets To Defend L
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Continued To May-05-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Forbes for Marcel Miller, attorney-in-fact for Michelle Benoit (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition to Compel Accounting, Demand Information, and Related Relief.

Mr. Forbes reported that trustee Diana Johnson had failed to comply with prior orders to provide trust documents and information and had not responded to counsel's repeated attempts to obtain the trust or communicate regarding administration. Mr. Forbes further reported that no signed copy of the trust had been produced and that efforts to obtain a death certificate for Kristine Knockum had been unsuccessful.

The Court noted that Ms. Johnson had not complied with prior directives and had failed to respond to counsel's outreach. On the Court's own motion, the Court suspended Diana Johnson as trustee.

The Court ordered that, no later than April 21, 2026, Ms. Johnson shall: (1) provide all trust documents and information regarding trust assets to petitioner's counsel; (2) file proof of bond, if required; and (3) file a copy of Kristine Knockum's death certificate.

The Court continued the matter to May 5, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and ordered that if Ms. Johnson fails to comply with the above directives, she will be removed as trustee at that time.

The Court waived defective notice for this hearing only, noting that Ms. Johnson had been present when the matter was previously set. The Court further advised counsel regarding proper notice requirements, including the need to include full remote appearance instructions with any future notice.

The Court directed Mr. Forbes to prepare the order.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Devon O'Shea appeared via zoom for Michelle Benoit.
Danielle Jones and Diana Johnson appeared via zoom (pro per).

Discussion was held regarding the trust, that the original is on MS. Johnson's possession, that it's at the 985 Oak st. residence, and that she will contact Ms. O'Shea to give her a copy of the original trust. The matter is continued to December 30, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported
